name: [BUG-XS-001] TC-XS-001 — Error response body schema differs across all three services
description:
**[BUG-XS-001]**

Each service returns a different JSON shape for error responses, making it impossible to write a single error-handling routine on the client.

| Service | Error Fields |
|---------|-------------|
| Analytics (405) | `{timestamp, status, error, path}` — no `message` field |
| Dashboard (401) | `{status, error, message}` — no `timestamp` or `path` |
| Transaction (401) | `{status, error, message}` — same as Dashboard |

**Expected:** Uniform error schema: `{status, error, message, timestamp, path}` across all services.

**Impact:** Frontend error-handling code must branch per-service. New services will likely add a 4th format. Clients cannot reliably extract a user-facing error message.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-XS-001]

Each service returns a different JSON shape for error responses, making it impossible to write a single error-handling routine on the client.

ServiceError Fields
Analytics (405){timestamp, status, error, path} — no message field
Dashboard (401){status, error, message} — no timestamp or path
Transaction (401){status, error, message} — same as Dashboard

Expected: Uniform error schema: {status, error, message, timestamp, path} across all services.

Impact: Frontend error-handling code must branch per-service. New services will likely add a 4th format. Clients cannot reliably extract a user-facing error message.
